body_txt: After much pleading, begging, pity, and a sense of moral justice, The Hero arrives, determined to save the townspeople from a terrible fate at the hands of a devious villain. Everything's looking all right, everyone is happy, and then — Bam. Betrayed. The Big Bad comes out of nowhere, captures The Hero, and tells the townspeople "You have done well to betray this interloper, for after your plans failed, I would have destroyed all of you." Note that the villain will probably still follow through with the original plans anyway , the complicity of the townspeople notwithstanding. " Dude, Where's My Respect? ", asks The Hero, as constantly being backstabbed, dismissed, obstructed, and taken for granted by the very people they are sworn to protect at risk of their very lives can be rather taxing on all cylinders. This trope also has some Fourth Wall Myopia in play - the townsfolk may seem ungrateful, but as far as they know, The Hero and the villain just whipped through town, caused who-knows-how-much property damage, and left them to clean up the mess and with the bill. See also: Apathetic Citizens , Les Collaborateurs , and Powder Keg Crowd . If they never liked the hero in the first place, they're probably All of the Other Reindeer .
